transcript.text: Hi, Sydney, I'm just here for moral support.
Yeah, appreciate it.
I don't generally run these calls, but, yeah.
That's nice.
My headphones decided to shut down.
I figured I'd give them five minutes to trickle in and then...
Follow up if they, did you see, the note, Ali's note takers here, yeah.
Thank
Thank you.
Thank you.
Two minutes late.
No worries.
Hi, Ali.
I don't think we've met yet.
I'm Andy.
I lead customer operations for GrowthX.
Ada's out of the office through the end of next week.
Today's her first day out.
And so I'm just here supporting Sydney, but she'll be leading.
So I thought I'd say hi.
Nice to meet you guys.
Yeah, likewise.
Where are you based?
I'm in Rhode Island.
How about you?
Atlanta.
Nice.
East Coast.
Yeah.
Yep.
Awesome.
So can we just get into it?
Yes, of course.
Are we going to wait for Alex?
Is he not going to join the call?
So I pinged him that I was running late, and I assumed he was going to be here already.
So I'm not sure exactly who it is, but we can just go ahead and get started.
Okay.
Awesome.
Okay.
So I'm just going to share my screen for you.
So this is our meeting agenda for the day.
So since last week, we have created.
We your OS, content OS for all of your ideas live.
We've set up your Scrunch dashboard, and we've generated some featured image blogs that I'll show you in a little bit.
On the blog design, we're still waiting.
We're still talking to our design team about how that's all going to happen.
So I'll get you a timeline as soon as I can on that.
So for the content OS, really quickly.
So last week, we presented the strategy for you, and all of these ideas align with that overall strategy.

So we have here what topic clusters we're covering, based on the strategy that we showed you last week, as well as the specific ideas, including the keywords that we're targeting, and then the volume and difficulty of those keywords.

So if you go through this proposed ideas list, this should have all the ideas that we would like for you to go through, and then tell us which ones you like, which ones you wanted to start with, which ones are priorities.
So in this column, the status column, you can just switch it over to approve to start.
And that means that we'll start generating those articles.
Okay.
Remind me again how I get here?
I added a link to our meeting notes, but I will also send the link to this in the chat.
Let's see.
Just wanted to add it.
But yeah, you can keep going.
This is great.
Okay.
Yeah.
Yeah, because I have to share a specific type of link so that you can access it from a creator status, which I have in the meeting notes, but I don't have it on hand currently.
But I can send it to chat in a little bit.
So yeah, so this should also include a short description and everything.
And then so as soon as you approve the ideas, we'll start generating the content.
Next week, we'll send over, early next week, we'll send over two calibration articles that we would like for you to go through and then give as much feedback as possible.
Like let us know if this aligns with tone, style, everything, the words that you like, that you don't like, all of that, so that we can feed it back into our content agents.
Engine, and then continue to improve on those first drafts, so that the next time, there won't be as many edits needed.
And it's also for our knowledge, for your editors, so that we know what you like and what you don't like, and so we can build out your content so much better.
Cool.
I'm in the docu or the sheet.
Just so I'm clear, you're waiting, you're going to wait for us to change the status, or what exactly do you want us to do on each row?
Yeah, just tell me if you like it, by changing it to approve to start.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, and then we'll take it from there.
Awesome.

And then if there's anything that you want us to prioritize, like if there's an idea that screams out to you, like, yes, we should do this first, feel free to add a comment in the record, so you can expand and add a comment on the side, or you can just pay me in Slack, if that's easier, I mean.
Sounds good.
And then, so I've set up your content OS, so that you can see the different statuses of all of our ideas, so we have the by status by cluster tab, and that way you can see,
Which ideas belong to which cluster easier?

Proposed ideas, we'll have all the ideas we have that you've not approved, and then when we start working on them, you'll see them in the content in progress, and then when it's ready for review, we'll go in, content ready for review.
This is all just to make your lives easier, but if it's complicated at all, or we want to change how the system is set up, let me know, or if you want me to add a new view, works as well.
Okay, and then, so, we have also set up your Scrunch dashboard for LLM tracking.
I know that we didn't, we, we walked you through Atlas last week, and so this week will be your Scrunch dashboard walkthrough, so let me know if you've already seen this before.

But, so we've set up a couple of prompts here, based on what you told us, you valued, and what you want to get cited on from LLMs, so data-driven marketing analytics, B2B software marketing strategies, effective.
Old email outreach techniques, all of these have specific prompts in them.
So like this one, who can help with data-driven marketing strategies for B2B software firms.
Ideally, would like understory to show up there, but there are like competitive presence, like it's not citing anyone so far.
this is actually something that we can definitely target.
And some of the keywords in your content OS are built around these prompts specifically.
So currently what it's doing is here, sharing individuals is what is happening.
So I remember you mentioned last week that you would like for your leadership team to show up in these prompts as well.
So that's something that we could do, or we could try to target based on like what we're seeing here.
So we are seeing directive consulting is here, sorry, Refined Labs.
I don't know why it's saying that competitive presence isn't here.
But anyway, so Refined Labs is here, directive consulting is here.
So ideally you would like understory to appear on.

So that means we'll look through these sites, and we can send these over to you if you want to do outreach on these sites that cite the other agencies, and we can also create our own listicles to target these.

So every week we'll look through this list of prompts that we have and use it for content ideas, and if we have very specific ones that we see that, hey, this is actually a really good idea, we'll send that over to you immediately and ask for approval so we can start the content creation process on that earlier.
I'll also send you, after this call, a list of all of the prompts so that you can go through them and then see if you want to add more.
Like, it's nothing urgent, we already have all the prompts set up, but just in case you wanted to add more prompts or you wanted to edit some of them, we can do that as well.
So how do I access this?
You say you send an invite to Scrum, or do we get, like, access to this dashboard?
So this one, yeah, Andy, you can jump in here.
Yeah.
Yeah.
At the moment, we don't because of the way that they recognize email aliases in a certain account.
Scrunch is like still a pretty young company.
So like GrowthX, you have to have a GrowthX.ai email, I think, for us to add you at this point.
But we can take a screenshot every week and share or go over in this call every week just to see.
And I mean, like we set up this dashboard, I think, on Monday.
So the movement over time is really like you're not going to see anything in a day or two days.
It's like it'll keep pinging and asking those questions of the prompts over, you know, a week or two.
Okay.
All right, makes sense.
Yeah, I actually got intro'd to the founder at some point, but we never got to connect.
I guess it's worth scheduling something and maybe telling him about the email thing.
But this is pretty cool.
I'm excited to start ranking for stuff.
Yeah.
All
be fun to see where we can get you with the listicles and which listicles are actually working and continue to iterate from there.
It's just, it's nice to have that data to see where we can improve and where we can get better.
For sure.
Okay.
And then I also wanted to show you what we currently have set up for your pipeline.
Sorry.
This.
So Ada showed you this last week, how we generate the articles.
We have added a step here at the very end where we'll generate an icon and then it will overlay onto basically an image based on the brand guidelines that you send us.
And these are the current proposals that we have for this article.
So we've got that kind of icon or this one works as well.
And if this looks good to you, like I can send these to you as well, like in a, in a separate file in Slack.
So you can look over it and tell us if it looks good, if you want anything changed, if this doesn't align.
And with your brand, all of those things.
I think they look good for the most part.
Be solid.
What do you think, Alex?
Okay.
So far, I mean, honestly, I don't love how dark that green is, but I think that's our brand guidelines at this point, isn't it?
I don't know.
Not the biggest deal in the world, though.
I'm not that picky.
Okay.

Well, I can, yeah, if you have any specific pieces of feedback, we can give it to the designer, because this is basically what happens is there's a background that's generated, and then the text gets overlaid on top.
So the background is actually not super hard to change if that's a little bit too dark.
I can ask her to do just a solid block of green and see if that looks better.
Yeah, I can send over a couple of ideas, and then you can tell me which one you like best, and then we can implement that across the board.
Sounds good.
Okay.
And then, so I think that was actually it on the agenda for the day.
So since you generate...
Sorry, approve ideas, we can start on your articles, and then get that first round of reviews in, and then start a regular cadence of delivering and publishing.
Awesome.
So I guess it's just on us, it's maybe scheduled some time to go over all the content, but I guess one question I had is, so on the WordPress end, how is this actually going to get deployed?
Like once we approve all the content, like have we made progress just in WordPress?
Is there anything you need for that?
So we're still trying to figure out how to build the blog page, and how much time that would take.

I think it'll take about two to three weeks to get that all done, but it would be nice to have like just a backlog of content that we can publish as soon as it's live, so we can do those two things simultaneously, and I'll continue to coordinate with our design team and see, give you a timeline as soon as possible.
Okay, sounds good, and whatever you recommend is the best solution, like we're pretty open at this point, like whatever it takes to get like a
Good blog, and something like if we decided to adjust the current structure of the website, wouldn't be too crazy to rebuild.
Okay.
So just keep that in mind.
Okay, awesome.
Yeah, I'll, yeah, I'm also talking to Jason about it, because I know you talked to Jason about this idea when you chatted earlier, is that correct?
I think so.
Yeah, so yeah, I am talking to Jason about how we can get this going, and, like, what his initial ideas are, were as well, just so that we're all, you know, aligned on that front.
Yeah, absolutely.
Awesome.
Awesome.
We'll start reviewing the content then.
Awesome, okay, and if you, do you have any questions for us at all?
I realize that we still have 15 minutes, but if I can give you back 15 minutes, I mean, that'd be great.
Nothing on my end so far.
Yeah, I'm all good.
Like I said, I think we just have to go through all the content, and I'm sure that's going to start to open up more questions and ideas.
Yeah.
Okay.
Awesome.
Thank you both so much for being here.
And I will see you next week.
Ada's out till next week.
So you'll get me and Andy until next week, and then Ada will be back.
Sounds good.
We have a meeting with you guys next week for the other stuff, too, that we're doing for you guys.
Oh, perfect.
Oh, awesome.
Yeah.
And we'll actually be working from your office as well in San Francisco.
So pretty exciting.
Oh, that's a really cool.
It is a nice.
There's good sunlight in there.
Nice.
Andy, where are you based?
I'm in Rhode Island.
I'm on the East Coast.
Okay.
Cool.
And Sydney, you're still in Seattle right now, right?
Yeah, Vancouver.
Yeah, Vancouver.
Yeah.
Yeah.
When are you going to?
I fly in two weeks.
Two weeks.
September 22.
So I think these calls will be at 12 a.m.
for me or something like that.
Rough.
I actually like it.
I mean, I tell people internet's a lot faster at night.
There you go.
That makes sense.
makes sense.
Although, I feel like so many people in the Philippines work at American companies, so I don't know.
They do, but not in the area that I'm in, so I'm not competing with them as much.
Okay, there you go.
Cool.
Yeah.
All right, so Ali and I will review everything, and we will get back to you.
Awesome, thank you so much.
Thanks, Ali.
right, guys, thank you.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync to discuss content strategy progress and next steps for Understory's blog development.](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=84.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content OS set up with proposed ideas; Understory to review and approve topics](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=298.0)
  - [Scrunch dashboard established for LLM tracking; GrowthX to share weekly updates](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=507.0)
  - [Blog design timeline pending; 2-3 weeks estimated for implementation](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=875.0)
  - [First round of content reviews to begin soon, establishing regular publishing cadence](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy Progress

  - [Content OS created with topic clusters, keywords, and difficulty metrics](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=298.0)
  - [Scrunch dashboard set up for LLM tracking (data-driven marketing, B2B software strategies, email outreach)](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=526.0)
  - [Featured image blog designs generated (icon overlays on brand-guideline backgrounds)](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
  - [Two calibration articles to be sent next week for detailed feedback](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)

### Content Approval Process

  - [Understory to review proposed ideas in Content OS](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
  - [Change status to "approve to start" for preferred topics](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=354.0)
  - [Add comments or Slack messages for prioritization](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
  - [Content statuses viewable by cluster and progress stage](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=470.0)

### LLM Tracking and Competitive Analysis

  - [Scrunch dashboard tracks specific prompts (e.g., "data-driven marketing strategies for B2B software firms")](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=544.0)
  - [Current results show individuals; aim to have Understory appear](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=548.0)
  - [Competitors like Refined Labs and Directive Consulting appearing in results](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=585.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide weekly screenshots/updates (no direct Understory access yet)](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=676.0)

### Blog Design and Implementation

  - [Timeline for blog page build-out pending (est. 2-3 weeks)](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)
  - [Coordinating with design team for best approach](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)
  - [Understory open to adjusting current website structure if needed](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=893.0)

### Featured Image Designs

  - [Icon overlays on brand guideline backgrounds presented](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=760.0)
  - [Minor feedback on dark green background; designer to provide alternatives](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=797.0)
  - [Sydney to send separate files for detailed review](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=770.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Understory to review and approve content ideas in Content OS](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
  - [GrowthX to send two calibration articles for feedback early next week](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)
  - [Sydney to provide alternative featured image designs with lighter backgrounds](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=817.0)
  - [GrowthX to share Scrunch dashboard prompts list for potential additions/edits](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=631.0)
  - [Sydney to follow up with Jason and design team on blog implementation timeline](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=868.0)
  - [Next meeting scheduled; Ada returns the following week](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=967.0)
  - [Understory team to work from GrowthX San Francisco office next week](https://fathom.video/share/yyzHjqYNx3RhnLc8Tj8PpdGt7Kwc3oBh?tab=summary&timestamp=979.0)

action_items.description: Send Content OS link w/ creator access to Ali & Alex via chat
Send 2 calibration articles early next week for detailed feedback on tone, style, wording
Review Content OS ideas, approve to start by changing status, prioritize w/ comments
Review Content OS ideas, approve to start by changing status, prioritize w/ comments
Send list of LLM tracking prompts to Ali & Alex for review/additions
Send featured image proposals via Slack for review
Create alt bg options for featured imgs (incl. solid green block), send for feedback
Coordinate w/ design team, provide timeline for blog page dev (est. 2-3 weeks)
